page_content:
{"Question": "How do I aggregate time series data by different time periods?", "Context": "I have daily sales data and want to aggregate it into weekly and monthly summaries for trend analysis.", "Code_Example": "from trendanalysistools import TrendPipe, aggregate_by_time

# Create pipeline with your data
pipeline = TrendPipe.from_dataframe(sales_df)

# Aggregate by week
weekly_data = pipeline | aggregate_by_time(
  date_column='sale_date',
  metric_columns=['revenue', 'units_sold'],
  time_period='W', # 'W' for weekly
  aggregation='sum', # Sum the metrics for each week
  fill_missing=True # Fill missing weeks with NaN
)

# Aggregate by month
monthly_data = pipeline | aggregate_by_time(
  date_column='sale_date',
  metric_columns=['revenue', 'units_sold'],
  time_period='M', # 'M' for monthly
  aggregation='sum'
)

# Access the aggregated data
print(weekly_data.time_aggregations['time'].data.head())", "Usage": "aggregate_by_time(date_column='date', metric_columns=['value'], time_period='M', aggregation='sum', fill_missing=True)"}

metadata.description: Aggregate data by different time periods (daily, weekly, monthly, etc.)

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I calculate growth rates between time periods?", "Context": "I want to calculate month-over-month and year-over-year growth rates for my revenue data.", "Code_Example": "from trendanalysistools import TrendPipe, aggregate_by_time, calculate_growth_rates

# First aggregate data by month
pipeline = TrendPipe.from_dataframe(revenue_df)
monthly_data = pipeline | aggregate_by_time(
  date_column='transaction_date',
  metric_columns=['revenue'],
  time_period='M'
)

# Calculate month-over-month growth rates
mom_growth = monthly_data | calculate_growth_rates(
  window=1, # Compare to previous month
  annualize=False, # Don't annualize the rates
  method='percentage' # Calculate as percentage change
)

# Calculate year-over-year growth rates
yoy_growth = monthly_data | calculate_growth_rates(
  window=12, # Compare to 12 months ago
  annualize=False,
  method='percentage'
)

# Access the growth rate data
growth_df = mom_growth.trend_results['time_growth']['data']
print(growth_df.head())", "Usage": "calculate_growth_rates(window=12, annualize=False, method='percentage')"}

metadata.description: Calculate growth rates between time periods

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I smooth out noisy time series data?", "Context": "My daily sales data is very noisy with lots of ups and downs. I want to smooth it to see the underlying trend.", "Code_Example": "from trendanalysistools import TrendPipe, aggregate_by_time, calculate_moving_average

# Create pipeline with daily data
pipeline = TrendPipe.from_dataframe(daily_sales_df)
daily_data = pipeline | aggregate_by_time(
  date_column='sale_date',
  metric_columns=['revenue', 'units_sold'],
  time_period='D'
)

# Calculate 7-day simple moving average
seven_day_ma = daily_data | calculate_moving_average(
  window=7,
  method='simple',
  center=False # Align at the end of window
)

# Calculate 30-day exponential moving average (gives more weight to recent values)
thirty_day_ema = daily_data | calculate_moving_average(
  window=30,
  method='exponential'
)

# Access the moving average data
ma_df = seven_day_ma.trend_results['time_ma']['data']
print(ma_df.head())", "Usage": "calculate_moving_average(window=7, method='simple', center=False)"}

metadata.description: Smooth time series data using moving averages

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I decompose a time series into trend, seasonal, and residual components?", "Context": "I want to separate the seasonal patterns from the overall trend in my monthly sales data.", "Code_Example": "from trendanalysistools import TrendPipe, aggregate_by_time, decompose_trend

# First aggregate to monthly data
pipeline = TrendPipe.from_dataframe(sales_df)
monthly_data = pipeline | aggregate_by_time(
  date_column='sale_date',
  metric_columns=['revenue'],
  time_period='M'
)

# Decompose the time series
decomposed = monthly_data | decompose_trend(
  metric_column='revenue',
  model='additive', # Use additive model (trend + seasonal + residual)
  period=12, # 12 months per year
  extrapolate_trend=6 # Extrapolate trend 6 months into future
)

# Access the decomposition components
decomp_name = 'time_revenue_decomp'
trend = decomposed.trend_decompositions[decomp_name]['trend']
seasonal = decomposed.trend_decompositions[decomp_name]['seasonal']
residual = decomposed.trend_decompositions[decomp_name]['residual']
print(f'Trend values: {trend.head()}')", "Usage": "decompose_trend(metric_column='revenue', model='additive', period=12, extrapolate_trend=6)"}

metadata.description: Decompose time series into trend, seasonal, and residual components

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I forecast future values in a time series?", "Context": "I need to predict sales for the next 12 months based on historical data.", "Code_Example": "from trendanalysistools import TrendPipe, aggregate_by_time, forecast_metric

# First aggregate to monthly data
pipeline = TrendPipe.from_dataframe(sales_df)
monthly_data = pipeline | aggregate_by_time(
  date_column='sale_date',
  metric_columns=['revenue'],
  time_period='M'
)

# Forecast future values using Holt-Winters method
forecasted = monthly_data | forecast_metric(
  metric_column='revenue',
  fperiods=12, # Forecast 12 periods ahead
  fmethod='holt_winters', # Use Holt-Winters exponential smoothing
  seasonal_periods=12, # Annual seasonality (12 months)
  confidence_interval=0.95 # 95% confidence interval
)

# Access the forecast
forecast_name = 'time_revenue_forecast'
forecast_df = forecasted.forecasts[forecast_name]['data']
print(forecast_df.head())", "Usage": "forecast_metric(metric_column='revenue', fperiods=12, fmethod='holt_winters', seasonal_periods=12, confidence_interval=0.95)"}

metadata.description: Forecast future values of time series data

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I test if a trend in my data is statistically significant?", "Context": "I think my website traffic is increasing, but I want to confirm if the upward trend is statistically significant.", "Code_Example": "from trendanalysistools import TrendPipe, aggregate_by_time, calculate_statistical_trend

# First aggregate to daily data
pipeline = TrendPipe.from_dataframe(traffic_df)
daily_data = pipeline | aggregate_by_time(
  date_column='visit_date',
  metric_columns=['visitors', 'page_views'],
  time_period='D'
)

# Test for statistically significant trend
trend_test = daily_data | calculate_statistical_trend(
  metric_column='visitors',
  test_method='mann_kendall', # Use Mann-Kendall test (non-parametric)
  alpha=0.05, # 5% significance level
  window=None # Test entire series
)

# Also calculate rolling trend (30-day windows)
rolling_trend = daily_data | calculate_statistical_trend(
  metric_column='visitors',
  test_method='mann_kendall',
  alpha=0.05,
  window=30 # Test in 30-day rolling windows
)

# Check the results
trend_name = 'time_visitors_trend'
print(f"Trend: {trend_test.trend_results[trend_name]['trend']}")
print(f"Significant: {trend_test.trend_results[trend_name]['significant']}")
print(f"p-value: {trend_test.trend_results[trend_name]['p_value']}")", "Usage": "calculate_statistical_trend(metric_column='visitors', test_method='mann_kendall', alpha=0.05, window=None)"}

metadata.description: Test if a trend is statistically significant

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I compare time periods like year-over-year or month-over-month?", "Context": "I want to compare this month's performance with the same month last year and with last month.", "Code_Example": "from trendanalysistools import TrendPipe, aggregate_by_time, compare_periods

# First aggregate to monthly data
pipeline = TrendPipe.from_dataframe(sales_df)
monthly_data = pipeline | aggregate_by_time(
  date_column='sale_date',
  metric_columns=['revenue', 'units_sold'],
  time_period='M'
)

# Compare to previous month (month-over-month)
mom_comparison = monthly_data | compare_periods(
  metric_column='revenue',
  comparison_type='month_over_month',
  n_periods=1, # Compare to 1 month ago
  aggregation='mean'
)

# Compare to same month last year (year-over-year)
yoy_comparison = monthly_data | compare_periods(
  metric_column='revenue',
  comparison_type='year_over_year',
  n_periods=1, # Compare to 1 year ago
  aggregation='mean'
)

# Access the comparison results
comp_name = 'time_revenue_comparison'
comp_df = mom_comparison.trend_results[comp_name]['data']
print(comp_df[['current', 'previous', 'abs_diff', 'rel_diff']].tail())", "Usage": "compare_periods(metric_column='revenue', comparison_type='year_over_year', n_periods=1, aggregation='mean')"}

metadata.description: Compare metrics between different time periods

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I identify which metrics are growing the fastest or have the strongest trends?", "Context": "I'm tracking 20 different metrics and want to know which ones have the strongest growth or most significant trends.", "Code_Example": "from trendanalysistools import TrendPipe, aggregate_by_time, calculate_growth_rates, calculate_statistical_trend, get_top_metrics

# First aggregate and calculate growth and trends for multiple metrics
pipeline = TrendPipe.from_dataframe(business_df)
monthly_data = pipeline | aggregate_by_time(
  date_column='date',
  metric_columns=['revenue', 'users', 'conversion_rate', 'ads_spend', 'profit'],
  time_period='M'
)

# Calculate growth rates for all metrics
growth_data = monthly_data | calculate_growth_rates(window=3)

# Calculate trend significance for all metrics
trend_data = monthly_data | calculate_statistical_trend(
  metric_column='revenue',
  test_method='mann_kendall'
) | calculate_statistical_trend(
  metric_column='users',
  test_method='mann_kendall'
) | calculate_statistical_trend(
  metric_column='conversion_rate',
  test_method='mann_kendall'
) | calculate_statistical_trend(
  metric_column='ads_spend',
  test_method='mann_kendall'
) | calculate_statistical_trend(
  metric_column='profit',
  test_method='mann_kendall'
)

# Get top 3 metrics by growth rate
top_growth = get_top_metrics(
  n=3,
  metric_type='growth',
  ascending=False # Get highest growth first
)(growth_data)

# Get top 3 metrics by trend strength
top_trends = get_top_metrics(
  n=3,
  metric_type='trend',
  ascending=False # Get strongest positive trends first
)(trend_data)

print('Top growth metrics:
', top_growth)
print('
Top trending metrics:
', top_trends)", "Usage": "get_top_metrics(n=5, metric_type='growth', ascending=False)"}

metadata.description: Identify top metrics based on growth, trend strength, or other criteria

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I create a complete trend analysis pipeline for my sales data?", "Context": "I want to analyze my sales data, including aggregation, growth rates, seasonality, forecasting, and identifying significant trends.", "Code_Example": "from trendanalysistools import TrendPipe, aggregate_by_time, calculate_growth_rates, calculate_moving_average, decompose_trend, forecast_metric, calculate_statistical_trend

# Create pipeline with your sales data
pipeline = TrendPipe.from_dataframe(sales_df)

# Create a complete trend analysis pipeline
analysis = (pipeline
  # Aggregate to monthly data
  | aggregate_by_time(
      date_column='sale_date',
      metric_columns=['revenue', 'units_sold', 'customer_count'],
      time_period='M'
    )
  # Calculate growth rates
  | calculate_growth_rates(
      window=1, # Month-over-month
      method='percentage'
    )
  | calculate_growth_rates(
      window=12, # Year-over-year
      method='percentage'
    )
  # Smooth the data
  | calculate_moving_average(
      window=3,
      method='simple'
    )
  # Decompose trends
  | decompose_trend(
      metric_column='revenue',
      model='additive',
      period=12
    )
  # Forecast future values
  | forecast_metric(
      metric_column='revenue',
      fperiods=6,
      fmethod='holt_winters',
      seasonal_periods=12
    )
  # Check statistical significance
  | calculate_statistical_trend(
      metric_column='revenue',
      test_method='mann_kendall'
    )
)

# Now you can access all the components of the analysis
print("Monthly data:", analysis.time_aggregations['time']['data'].head())
print("Growth rates:", analysis.trend_results['time_growth']['data'].head())
print("Moving averages:", analysis.trend_results['time_ma']['data'].head())
print("Trend decomposition:", analysis.trend_decompositions['time_revenue_decomp']['trend'].head())
print("Forecast:", analysis.forecasts['time_revenue_forecast']['data'].head())
print("Trend significance:", analysis.trend_results['time_revenue_trend']['significant'])", "Usage": "pipeline | aggregate_by_time(...) | calculate_growth_rates(...) | decompose_trend(...) | forecast_metric(...)"}

metadata.description: Create a complete trend analysis pipeline using method chaining
